name: Joan's Little Joys
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Finely ground hazelnuts or macadamia nuts
Food coloring as desired, for decorating
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Grease a cookie sheet or line with parchment paper.
2. In a large bowl, beat butter and 3/4 cup confectioners' sugar until well combined. Mix in vanilla extract.
3. Add flour and ground nuts; mix on low speed just until combined.
4. Roll dough into small balls (about 1 teaspoon each) and place on the prepared baking sheet.
5. Dip a flat glass in confectioners' sugar, then press each ball to flatten to about 1-inch round.
6. Bake for 9-11 minutes or until lightly colored. Cool on a wire rack.
7. Spread preserves on half of the cookies. Sandwich with another cookie.
8. For icing: Mix remaining confectioners' sugar with enough water to reach spreading consistency. Add food coloring if desired.
9. Decorate top of cookies with icing using a pastry bag, if desired.
Fifth-place winner in the 1995 Chicago Tribune Holiday Cookie Contest: by Isabelle Clarke Garibaldi of Burr Ridge
